library_meta.name: IMS Isometric Ramping Library

methodology.what_is_iso_ramping: Isometric ramping is a graded-intensity isometric protocol where the client progressively increases effort from 10% through 100% of maximum voluntary contraction, either across multiple reps or within a single rep. The muscle or capsular structure is held at a specific position (long/stretched OR short/shortened) while effort ramps. Unlike a simple iso hold, the ramp trains the neurological recruitment curve and the tissue's load tolerance at graduated thresholds.

long_vs_short_iso.long_iso: Long iso = isometric contraction performed at the LENGTHENED / STRETCHED position. Example: in 90/90, pushing front shin into floor while the hip is in full ER (the stretched position of the capsule). Primarily trains capsular load tolerance and end-range strength. Routes to ISM Inputs 1 and 3.
long_vs_short_iso.short_iso: Short iso = isometric contraction performed at the SHORTENED / CONTRACTED position. Example: in 90/90, actively lifting the front shin and holding it at end-range active ER (the shortened position of the external rotators). Primarily trains active end-range strength and neurological recruitment. Routes to ISM Input 4 for fast twitch and Input 3 for connective tissue load bearing.

two_use_cases.tissue_prep: Ramping used BEFORE heavy loaded work to prime the tissue and neurological system. Fast ramp (10-100% in 30-45 seconds total), short rest, 2-3 rounds. Purpose: wake up the motor units, prepare the capsule for loading, establish neurological access to end-range force production. Done early in a session before strength or power work.
two_use_cases.spasm_relaxation: Ramping used to DOWN-REGULATE a guarded or spasmed muscle. Slow ramp (10-100% over 60-90 seconds), long holds at each tier, 3-5 rounds. Purpose: use graded contraction-relaxation cycles to exhaust the guarded tissue and allow it to release. Done when assessment identifies spasm as the primary restrictor. Often paired with breath work and follows the PAILs/RAILs pattern but with more tiers of effort.

the_ramp_tiers.10_percent: Just initiation. Barely noticeable contraction. Wake up the motor unit.
the_ramp_tiers.25_percent: Light engagement. Client should feel the muscle turn on cleanly.
the_ramp_tiers.50_percent: Moderate effort. Sustained contraction without breath holding.
the_ramp_tiers.75_percent: Hard effort. Client is working. Still controlling the breath.
the_ramp_tiers.100_percent: Maximum voluntary contraction. Brief - 2-3 seconds maximum. Full recruitment.

methodology.ism_integration: Iso ramping primarily addresses Inputs 1 (Capsular Space - via long iso at stretched positions), Input 2 (CT Architecture - via ramped loading that organizes tissue), and Input 3 (Load Bearing Capacity - via progressive load tolerance). Spasm-relaxation protocols directly address guarded tissue that limits all other input work from being accessible.

library_meta.organization: By base position, then by joint + iso type (long/short)
library_meta.integration_with_assessment: Iso ramping is the primary prescription for assessment findings of Input 1 (Capsular Space) when the capsule is guarded, Input 2 (CT Architecture) when graded loading is needed, and Input 3 (Load Bearing) when tissue tolerance needs to be built. Spasm-protocol ramping is used when the assessment reveals guarding/protective patterns limiting access to other work. Tissue-prep protocol is used pre-session before loaded strength work at the flagged joint.

builder_integration.session_placement: Base Position Block (early) for tissue prep, OR dedicated slot for spasm work
builder_integration.typical_dose: 2-3 rounds for prep, 3-5 rounds for spasm relaxation
builder_integration.time_cost: 3-8 minutes per joint depending on rounds and rest

iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.name: 90/90 Front Hip ER Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.setup: Client seated in 90/90 position, front leg externally rotated at 90 degrees flexion (shin parallel to front of mat). This IS the stretched position of the anterior hip capsule / external rotators at end range ER.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.description: Client presses front shin DOWN into the floor - contracting the external rotators against the stretched position. The effort ramps through tiers. This is a long iso because the muscle is lengthened while contracting.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp. 10% → 25% → 50% → 75% → 100% across 5 reps. Each effort level held 3-5 seconds. 10 seconds rest between tiers. One full ramp ladder per round.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp. 10% held 10s → rest 20s → 25% held 10s → rest 20s → 50% held 10-15s → rest 30s → 75% held 10s → rest 30s → 100% held 5s. Focus on complete relaxation between tiers. Breath into the position on each rest.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.dose_prep: 2-3 rounds of the full ladder. 60 seconds rest between rounds.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.dose_spasm: 3-5 rounds. 90 seconds rest between rounds. Stop if spasm releases cleanly.
indications: PRE-heavy squat or lunge session (tissue prep)
Hip flexor / ER spasm or guarding (spasm relaxation)
Input 1 Capsular restriction that needs loaded approach
Transitioning client from PAIL/RAIL L2 to L3
pairs_with: Follow with loaded hip work (goblet squat, RFESS) if tissue prep use
Precede with breathwork and follow with 90/90 active CARs if spasm use
Pairs with hip_prh_er_90_90_front as the baseline
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_long.coach_notes: The tell for a properly-ramped iso: client's breath stays even through all tiers. If breath locks at 50%+, the ramp is too steep or the position is too intense - regress to a modified 90/90 with elevated sit bones. For spasm relaxation, watch for visible tissue release in the glute and hip flexor complex - that's the signal the protocol is working.

iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.name: 90/90 Back Hip IR Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.setup: Client seated in 90/90, back leg internally rotated. Thigh rests out to the side at end range IR.
iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.description: Client presses back thigh DOWN into the floor - contracting the internal rotators against the stretched position. Long iso because the IR tissues are lengthened while contracting.
iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp. 10-100% across 5 reps, 3-5 second holds, 10 second rests. One ladder per round.
iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp. Same structure as front leg protocol but 10-15 second holds at each tier, full relaxation rests.
indications: Hip IR restriction (very common in desk-bound adults)
Pre-squat prep for clients with hip IR deficit
Input 1 capsular restriction on posterior hip
Following a PAIL/RAIL block - higher-demand progression
iso_ramp_9090_back_ir_long.coach_notes: Back leg IR is harder for most clients than front leg ER. Expect weaker contractions at the high tiers - don't push to 100% if form breaks. 75% clean beats 100% with compensation every time.

iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.name: 90/90 Front Hip ER Short Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.setup: Client seated in 90/90. Front shin lifts slightly off floor via active external rotation - this is the SHORTENED position of the ER muscles (they're actively contracting to hold the lifted position).
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.description: From the lifted-shin position, client ramps effort upward - trying to lift the shin HIGHER / contract the ERs HARDER. This is a short iso because the muscle is in its shortened position while contracting.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp. 10-100% across 5 efforts at 3-second holds each. The 'effort' here = how hard to lift the shin. 10% = just barely floating it. 100% = maximum active ER.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.ramp_protocol_spasm: Not typically used for spasm - short isos are more for capacity building than spasm release. If used, slower ramp with longer rests.
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.dose_spasm: Not recommended - use long iso for spasm work
indications: Client has passive ER but lacks active end-range strength
Input 4 Fast Twitch deficit at hip ER
Building active CARs from passive range
Converting PRLO work to capacity work
contraindications: Cannot lift shin at all (start with PRLO first)
iso_ramp_9090_front_er_short.coach_notes: Short iso ramping is the hardest variant. Most clients will struggle to produce clean force at the higher tiers from this shortened position. That's expected and IS the training stimulus. Don't force 100% - the ramp itself is the work.

iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.name: Pigeon Front Hip ER Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.setup: Client in pigeon position, front leg externally rotated and flexed. Can be upright or in forward fold. Deeper loading of the anterior capsule than 90/90.
iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.description: Press front shin into floor - contracting ER against stretched position. More intense than 90/90 variant because the position loads deeper.
iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Moderate ramp. 25% → 50% → 75% → 100% across 4 efforts, 3-4 second holds. Start at 25% because pigeon is already a loaded position - 10% is essentially passive.
iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp with long rests. 25% → 50% → 75% with 15s holds, 30s rest, multiple rounds. Pair with breath at each rest.
indications: Pre-session tissue prep for clients doing heavy squatting
Hip flexor / ER spasm release (most effective position for this)
Input 1 + Input 3 combined deficit at hip
iso_ramp_pigeon_front_er_long.coach_notes: Pigeon iso ramping is intense. Use sparingly - 1-2x per week maximum. Excellent for spasm relaxation when the glute/piriformis is guarding. Watch for cramping in the glute at 75%+ effort - that's often the spasm breaking.

iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.name: Butterfly Adductor Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.setup: Client in butterfly position, soles of feet together, knees falling to sides at end range of adductor length.
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.description: Client actively presses knees DOWN to the floor - this contracts the adductors AT their lengthened position. Long iso for the adductor chain.
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp. 10-100% across 5 efforts, 3-5s holds, 10s rests.
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for adductor release. 10% → 25% → 50% → 75% with 10-15s holds, full relaxation rests. Breath into adductors during rests.
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_long.coach_notes: Adductor ramping is the most teachable iso ramp - clients immediately feel the muscle contracting at length. Great introduction to the concept of iso ramping for new clients.

iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_short.name: Butterfly Abduction Short Iso Ramp (Opposite Direction)
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_short.setup: Client in butterfly position. Knees AT end range of drop to floor.
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_short.description: Client actively pulls knees FURTHER toward the floor using abductor contraction - shortening the abductors at their end range. Short iso for hip abductors.
indications: Active abduction at end range deficit
Pairing with butterfly long iso for full reciprocal work
iso_ramp_butterfly_adductor_short.coach_notes: Pair butterfly long iso (pressing knees into floor) and butterfly short iso (pulling knees to floor) in the same block - they train opposite sides of the same joint. PAIL/RAIL-style reciprocal loading.

iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.name: Straddle Adductor Long Iso Ramp (Straight Leg)
iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.setup: Client seated in straddle, legs extended wide, knees straight, feet flexed. More intense adductor load than butterfly due to extended knees.
iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.description: Client presses legs DOWN into floor - contracting adductors at full extension. More intense than butterfly due to the extended-knee position.
iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp 10-100% across 5 efforts, 3-5s holds, 10s rests.
iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp. 10% → 25% → 50% → 75% with 10-15s holds. Great for chronic adductor guarding.
iso_ramp_straddle_adductor_long.coach_notes: Straddle ramping is more intense than butterfly - use with clients who have adequate passive range and need to build tissue tolerance at that range.

iso_ramp_halfkneeling_hip_flexor_long.name: Half-Kneeling Hip Flexor Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_hip_flexor_long.setup: Half-kneeling position. Back leg in hip extension, hip flexor at lengthened position.
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_hip_flexor_long.description: Client actively drives back knee INTO the floor - contracting hip flexors at their lengthened position. Long iso for the anterior hip / hip flexor complex.
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_hip_flexor_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for hip flexor spasm release. Common protocol. 10 → 25 → 50 → 75% with 15s holds. Extremely effective for psoas-dominant back pain.
indications: Chronic hip flexor tightness / psoas guarding
Desk-bound clients with anterior hip restriction
Lower back pain with hip flexor contribution
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_hip_flexor_long.coach_notes: One of the most useful iso ramps for the IMS population. Desk-bound 40+ year olds often have psoas guarding driving low back issues - this ramps down the psoas through graded contraction.

iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.name: Couch Stretch Quad Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.setup: Client in couch stretch. Back knee on floor, foot up against wall/couch. Tall torso, active glute.
iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.description: Client actively presses back foot INTO the wall - contracting the quad / rec fem at its lengthened position. Long iso for the quad complex.
iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Moderate ramp. 25% → 50% → 75% → 100% across 4 efforts, 3-5s holds. Starts higher because couch stretch is already intense.
iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp. 25 → 50 → 75% with 15-20s holds. For chronic quad/hip flexor guarding.
indications: Pre-squat session if quad is the limiter
iso_ramp_couch_quad_long.coach_notes: Use sparingly - couch stretch ramping is one of the most intense isos in the library. 1x/week maximum for most clients. Excellent post-heavy-squat session.

iso_ramp_deep_squat_long.name: Deep Squat Hip Flexion Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_deep_squat_long.setup: Deep squat passive hold. Heels flat, hips below knees, torso upright.
iso_ramp_deep_squat_long.description: From the passive hold, client drives feet INTO the floor while trying to stand - contracting the hip extensors at their lengthened position. Long iso at deepest squat position.
iso_ramp_deep_squat_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Not typically spasm-protocol - use for capacity building
indications: Input 3 Load Bearing at deep hip flexion
iso_ramp_deep_squat_long.coach_notes: Advanced. Only program for clients who own the deep squat for 60s+ passively. The ramp itself is a strength-at-end-range stimulus that transfers directly to heavy squatting.

iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.name: Supine 90/90 Shoulder ER Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.setup: Supine, shoulder abducted 90 degrees, elbow 90, forearm at end range of external rotation (back of hand on floor or block).
iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.description: Client presses back of hand DOWN into floor - contracting the external rotators against the stretched anterior capsule. Long iso for the shoulder anterior capsule.
iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp. 10-100% across 5 efforts, 3s holds, 10s rests.
iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp. 10 → 25 → 50 → 75% with 10-15s holds, 30s rests. Great for chronic anterior shoulder guarding.
indications: Anterior shoulder capsule restriction (common in lifters)
iso_ramp_supine_shoulder_er_long.coach_notes: Essential pre-work for heavy pressing. 2 minutes of this before a bench session can mean the difference between a healthy shoulder and a flared one. Don't skip with clients who press heavy.

iso_ramp_standing_shoulder_flex_long.name: Wall Shoulder Flexion Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_standing_shoulder_flex_long.setup: Standing back to wall, arms overhead in goalpost against wall, elbows and wrists pressed to wall at end range of shoulder flexion. Ribs down, glutes on.
iso_ramp_standing_shoulder_flex_long.description: Client presses arms INTO wall - contracting shoulder flexors at their lengthened overhead position. Long iso.
iso_ramp_standing_shoulder_flex_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for lat / pec minor guarding. 10 → 25 → 50 → 75% with 15s holds.
contraindications: Cannot touch wall with elbows without lumbar arch
iso_ramp_standing_shoulder_flex_long.coach_notes: Teachable for any client. Pair with wall slides as a pre-session warm-up for any upper body day.

iso_ramp_quadruped_shoulder_ir_long.name: Quadruped Thread-the-Needle Shoulder IR Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_quadruped_shoulder_ir_long.setup: Quadruped with one arm threaded under body, palm up, in the posterior capsule loading position.
iso_ramp_quadruped_shoulder_ir_long.description: Client presses threaded arm DOWN into the floor - contracting the IR muscles against the stretched posterior capsule. Long iso at posterior shoulder.
iso_ramp_quadruped_shoulder_ir_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for posterior shoulder capsule work. 10-15s holds.
indications: Anterior shoulder irritation secondary to posterior tightness
iso_ramp_quadruped_shoulder_ir_long.coach_notes: Under-programmed. Posterior capsule work is the forgotten half of shoulder health - anterior tightness gets all the attention. Clients with chronic anterior shoulder issues often need posterior work more.

iso_ramp_quadruped_tspine_rotation_long.name: Quadruped T-Spine Rotation Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_quadruped_tspine_rotation_long.setup: Quadruped with one hand behind head. Rotated to open end range (elbow pointing up and out).
iso_ramp_quadruped_tspine_rotation_long.description: At end range of rotation, client contracts rotational muscles to drive HARDER into the open position - long iso at rotated end range.
iso_ramp_quadruped_tspine_rotation_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for T-spine stiffness from prolonged sitting. 10-15s holds at each tier.
contraindications: Shoulder pain in the behind-head position
iso_ramp_quadruped_tspine_rotation_long.coach_notes: Use with desk workers regularly. Pairs well with breathing at end range - the rotation + breath combination releases mid-back tension effectively.

iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.name: Supine Foam Roller T-Spine Extension Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.setup: Supine with foam roller at mid-thoracic. Hands behind head, extended over the roller.
iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.description: Client presses hands into back of head while maintaining extension position - contracting deep spinal extensors at their lengthened position. Long iso.
iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for chronic kyphotic postures. 10-15s holds. Pair with breath.
iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.dose_prep: 2 rounds per segment (mid, upper T-spine). 60s rest.
iso_ramp_supine_tspine_extension_long.coach_notes: Underutilized tool. Most clients just do passive foam roller work - adding the iso ramp makes it an active capsular and neurological drill. Far more effective.

iso_ramp_halfkneeling_ankle_df_long.name: Half-Kneeling Ankle DF Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_ankle_df_long.setup: Half-kneeling. Front ankle at end range DF (knee over or past toes, heel down).
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_ankle_df_long.description: Client actively drives heel INTO the floor / calf contracts against the DF position - long iso for the gastroc/soleus complex.
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_ankle_df_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for chronic calf tightness. 10-15s holds. Extremely effective for runners with calf guarding.
iso_ramp_halfkneeling_ankle_df_long.coach_notes: High-utility. Most adults need DF ramping. 2-3 minutes pre-squat session can change the bottom of every squat.

iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.name: Prone Sphinx Lumbar Extension Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.setup: Prone on forearms (sphinx). Lumbar extension position. Pelvis heavy on floor.
iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.description: Client actively lifts chest slightly MORE - contracting spinal extensors at the extended position. Gentle long iso. Respect spine caution.
iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.ramp_protocol_prep: GENTLE ramp. 10-50% only across 3 efforts, 3s holds, 30s rests. Do NOT go to 100% in the spine.
iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp but capped at 50% effort. 10 → 25 → 50% with 10s holds. Pair with breath.
iso_ramp_prone_lumbar_extension_long.coach_notes: CAP at 50% effort maximum. Spine is not meant for 100% effort ramping. Unlike hip or shoulder, spinal ramping stays conservative.

iso_ramp_long_sit_knee_extension_short.name: Long Sit Terminal Knee Extension Short Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_long_sit_knee_extension_short.setup: Long sit. Working leg fully extended with heel lifted slightly (terminal extension, shortened quad position).
iso_ramp_long_sit_knee_extension_short.description: Client ramps effort trying to lift heel HIGHER / contract quad HARDER in terminal extension - short iso for VMO and quad at its shortened range.
iso_ramp_long_sit_knee_extension_short.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp. 10-100% across 5 efforts. Range of motion is tiny - 1-2 inches of heel lift.
iso_ramp_long_sit_knee_extension_short.coach_notes: Rehab-focused primarily. Great for clients with nagging knee issues - pairs with single-leg squat progression to build VMO strength through range.

iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.name: Supine Chin Tuck Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.setup: Supine, back of head on floor, chin tucked toward chest (deep cervical flexors at contracted position).
iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.description: Client actively tucks chin harder - ramping effort in the cervical flexion pattern. Very gentle spine iso.
iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.ramp_protocol_prep: GENTLE ramp. 10-50% only. 3 efforts, 3s holds, 20s rests.
iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp 10 → 25 → 50% for cervical extensor release. 10-15s holds.
iso_ramp_supine_cervical_flexion_long.coach_notes: Cap at 50%. Cervical is NEVER a 100% tissue. Program cautiously and document client tolerance.

iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.name: Quadruped Wrist Extension Long Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.setup: Quadruped with palms flat, fingers forward. Wrist at end range of extension under bodyweight load.
iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.description: Client actively lifts fingers / contracts wrist flexors at the extended position - long iso for wrist flexors at their lengthened range.
iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.ramp_protocol_prep: Fast ramp. 25-100% across 4 efforts (skipping 10% as it's essentially passive). 3s holds.
iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.ramp_protocol_spasm: Slow ramp for wrist flexor guarding (common in desk workers / climbers). 25 → 50 → 75% with 10s holds.
iso_ramp_quadruped_wrist_ext_long.coach_notes: Quick add-on to any upper body warm-up. 60 seconds of wrist ramping before heavy pressing pays dividends.

iso_ramp_standing_foot_arch_short.name: Standing Short Foot Iso Ramp
iso_ramp_standing_foot_arch_short.setup: Standing barefoot. Actively shorten the foot (arch lifts, ball draws toward heel without toe curl).
iso_ramp_standing_foot_arch_short.description: Client ramps effort pulling the foot into a shorter position - short iso for foot intrinsics.
iso_ramp_standing_foot_arch_short.coach_notes: Underprogrammed. Foot intrinsics matter for balance, gait, and knee/hip health. Small ramp, big downstream effect.
